title: thymus ciliatus var. zygiformis
intro: Thymus ciliatus var. zygiformis, also known as creeping thyme, is a type of perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of Europe, known for its low-growing, spreading habit and tiny, fragrant leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The health benefits of Thymus ciliatus var. zygiformis include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress and anxiety, and aid in digestion, due to its high content of essential oils and antioxidants. Herbal preparations of Thymus ciliatus var. zygiformis include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions or simply enjoyed for their fragrance and flavor.
